title: ComboBoxOption Class
description: Represents a new option within the ComboBox.

parameters.name: element
parameters.description: The element for which to create ComboBoxOption control.

properties.name: readonly element: HTMLElement
properties.description: Gets the root element of the option.

properties.name: readonly elementIcon: HTMLElement
properties.description: Gets the Icon element of the option.

properties.name: readonly elementText: HTMLElement
properties.description: Gets the Text element of the option.

properties.name: readonly parent?: ComboBox
properties.description: Gets the container element for search input.

properties.name: readonly group?: ComboBoxGroup
properties.description: Gets the parent group of the option.

properties.name: disabled: boolean
properties.description: Gets or sets a value indicating whether interaction with the option is disabled.
remarks: If disabled, the option will not respond to mouse clicks or keyboard selection. However, programmatic selection via code is still allowed.

properties.name: selected: boolean
properties.description: Gets or sets whether the option is selected or not.

properties.name: outline: boolean
properties.description: Gets or sets the outline visibility of the option. This value has no effect if the option does not have a parent.
properties.remarks: Only one option may be outlined at any given time.

properties.name: hidden: boolean
properties.description: Gets or sets whether the option is hidden.
properties.remarks: If all options within a group are hidden, the group's visibility will be toggled to hidden as well.

properties.name: value: T
properties.description: Gets the assigned value of the option.

properties.name: text: string
properties.description: Gets or sets the display text of the option.

properties.name: description: string
properties.description: Gets or sets the display description of the option.

methods.name: remove(): void
methods.description: Removes the option from its parent. If it has no parent, calling <mark>remove()</mark> does nothing.

methods.name: matchKeywords(keywords: string[]): boolean
methods.description: Checks whether the option matches all specified keywords.

parameters.name: keywords
parameters.description: The array of keywords to match against.

methods.returns: Returns <mark>true</mark> if the option matches all specified keywords; otherwise, <mark>false</mark>.
